title: syzygium haniffii
intro: Syzygium haniffii, also known as the Haniff's tree or Malabar tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It is native to the Western Ghats in India and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Syzygium haniffii include its ability to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and act as a natural antacid, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. Syzygium haniffii is often used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
intro_study: According to "Plants (Basel, Switzerland)", syzygium haniffii benefits include being rich in antioxidant phenolic compounds that can act as multi-target neuroprotective agents against Alzheimer's disease.
